title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 October 2024.

items.title: First Three Episodes of **Like a Dragon: Yakuza** have released on Amazon Prime Video
bullets: Reaction so far has been overall mixed to negative with reviewers criticizing the fact it's "nothing like the games"

items.title: **Balatro**'s back with another collaboration card pack
bullets: Friends of Jimbo Pack 2 features cameos from The Binding of Isaac and Slay the Spire, alongside Cyberpunk 2077 and Stardew Valley
The Free update is available now on PC, PS, Switch, Android and iOS

items.title: The PC version of the **Croc **remaster is exclusive to GOG, will come with the original version
bullets: The remaster will feature enhanced HD graphics, modern controls (with an option to keep the original’s tank controls), an updated camera and retro video modes. However, players who prefer retro aesthetics will also have access to the original 1997 - PC version of the game, tweaked to work on modern hardware.
As well as the PC version on GOG, the Croc remaster will also be released on PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X and Switch

items.title: **Yooka-Replaylee** is coming to PS5, Xbox Series S/X and ‘Nintendo platforms’
**PlayStation Stars** will no longer give credit for money spent on subscriptons
bullets: Points will also only last just over a year

items.title: **Beyond Good & Evil 2** still in development, now with new creative director
bullets: Industry veteran Fawzi Mesmar is joining the Beyond Good and Evil 2 team as creative director. Taking to Linkedin, Mesmar announced his new role at Ubisoft Montpellier, having worked with the team previously during his last three years as Ubisoft’s VP of Editorial.

Mesmar is filling the role left by previous creative director Emile Morel, after he passed away suddenly last year. Prior to working at Ubisoft, Fawzi Mesmar worked at DICE as head of design, as well as design jobs at King and Gameloft.

items.title: Indie boxing game **Undisputed ** sells one million units in a week
bullets: Undisputed was initially released via Early Access in January 2023, before launching on PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X on October 11.
